title: Validity of the Single Processor Approach to Achieving Large Scale Computing Capabilities
summary: Amdahl's Law — why an infinite number of processors does not make a task arbitrarily fast; speedup is bounded by the sequential portion.

title: Eyeriss: An Energy-Efficient Reconfigurable Accelerator for Deep Convolutional Neural Networks
summary: A pioneering CNN accelerator that minimizes data movement via a three-level memory hierarchy and the Row-Stationary dataflow.

title: Why Systolic Architecture?
summary: Kung's foundational case for systolic architecture — simple, regular cells used repetitively, balancing computation with I/O.

title: DianNao: A Small-Footprint High-Throughput Accelerator for Ubiquitous Machine-Learning
summary: An early, widely-cited ML hardware accelerator — small-footprint and high-throughput. (Review in progress.)
